title: theme — model relationships
description: Three aggregates + one outbound reference to a co-located row (user_theme_preferences, owned by user module).

internal.description: A Theme references a preset. Cannot delete the preset while any Theme references it.
Deleting a theme cascade-deletes its overrides.
Reverse — used by MigrateThemeTokensOnPresetUpdateJob to find affected downstream themes.

outbound.note: BelongsToTenant trait handles the global scope + auto-fill on save.
Nullable — platform-provided presets carry tenant_id = NULL. Cascades on TenantErased for tenant custom presets.
Author of a tenant custom preset. RESTRICT so a user deletion is blocked while they own presets — those must transfer ownership first.

inbound.description: Downstream modules read via TokenResolver — no downstream FKs point at theme rows. TokenResolver is the ONLY documented consumer contract; feature packages call resolver.resolveTokens(tenantId, mode) rather than joining on the theme tables.
inbound.user_theme_preferences_note: The user_theme_preferences table (owned by the user module) references user_id but does NOT carry an FK to any theme table — the preference is orthogonal to the tenant's theme selection (a user can prefer 'dark' regardless of which dark theme the tenant serves).
